posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
no wine in two weeks, but met Tuesday with my small OC group for Coche Corton Charlie night. Food at the Loft in the Montage was ok and we handled our own wines, decent stemware provided.

No formal notes. Not many taste descriptors. Too much fun talking.

00 Dom rosé—very quiet mousse, a calm wine, strawberries and red fruit buttressed by its integrated acidity, a nice quaff.

90 Coche CC—struck match, a little honeyed and past peak from a bottle bought early and stored well. Nose far better than palate, gorgeous nose. By itself, this or the next wine would have been more pleasing.

94 Coche CC—for me virtually the equal of 90. Similar notes, shockingly. All of these CC had the struck match Coche trademark happening.

00 Coche CC—in the middle of the pack till it accelerated at the finish and really opened up. Steely and firm but generous. Great Corton Charlie.

2015 Coche CC—probably the best of these, still a baby but one that can run with and ahead of the pack. Great density and fruit.

2009 Ramonet Montrachet—trademark mint/rosemary and simply beautiful. Powerful but restrained wine, mouthwatering and great with room to run. Very different from Coche, of course, but able to hang easily with the big boys. Probably my favorite of the night, but I have a real soft spot for Ramonet.

Super night. Salon night is next month.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked:
Wow. Now that is what I call a dinner! Breathtaking line up.
One Coche CC makes for a great night, but four!!

posts.name: C. Keller
posts.cooked: epic

posts.cooked: Well done Alan.

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked: Weeknight wines.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: That’s some serious Dury Duty!

posts.name: Don Cornutt
posts.cooked: The 2015 Meursault village was a huge homerun for the house. Can only imagine how good the CC is. Nice night of great wines here.

posts.name: Scott Brunson
posts.cooked: Beautiful

posts.name: Daniel Bailey
posts.cooked: 3 CCC’s in one night is my record. 4 is clearly excessive.

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked: Ahhh…so you were there Doc! Saw the pics on IG. Amazing line up…don’t see too many people drinkin/posting the goldies much. Wish I lived closer…could have contributed a 93! Salon next month…I might just have to start house hunting!

I had the Coche-Dury Corton-Charlemagne 2015 at the domain in november 2017.
Very high rating : 19/20

I also had a stunning 1996 at the domain in march 2006 (18,5/20).

posts.name: Markus S
posts.cooked: Far drive to go for a little tasting.

posts.name: Brady Daniels
posts.cooked:
Great tasting!

My favorite piece of wine writing ever is centered around Coche CC. Back then, $700 sounded a bit excessive, now, not so much.

Slate Magazine – 27 Jan 07

My quest for a $700 bottle of wine.

This is a story about a bottle of wine. A very expensive but also very delicious bottle of wine—a wine that so captivated me when I first tasted it...

posts.cooked: Too bad about the 1990. I do think it’s beginning to fray around the edges a bit, unless you get very lucky. I had a bottle in 2019 that had never left Burgundy that was simply spectacular.

93 is a GREAT Coche CC. C’mon down and we will drink.

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked: Question for you Coche ballers…what would you consider the GOAT? Would assume it was a CC…but maybe other? I know JF says the MP is his fav? Limited experience…but I would say 2010 CC. You?

posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked:
Question for you Coche ballers…what would you consider the GOAT? Would assume it was a CC…but maybe other? I know JF says the MP is his fav? Limited experience…but I would say 2010 CC. You?

Haven’t had it, but friend says 92, I’d vote 2005, have a lone 2008 left in a friend’s cellar to drink together.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: Limited sample set but 92 CC was other worldly.

posts.name: Karl K@zaks
posts.cooked: I remember in 1999 in San Francisco buying Cochran CC off the rack at a wine store in San Francisco. Don’t remember the vintage, drank it that evening.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Question for you Coche ballers…what would you consider the GOAT? Would assume it was a CC…but maybe other? I know JF says the MP is his fav? Limited experience…but I would say 2010 CC. You?

It depends on the vintage. In 2001 for example I would go with MP over CC, but it’s a matter of fine distinctions.

Best somewhat mature CCs I have drunk have been 1990, 1996, 1999 and 2008. Never had a perfect bottle of the 2005, though I live in hope.

posts.name: Craig Gleason
posts.cooked:
I remember in 1999 in San Francisco buying Cochran CC off the rack at a wine store in San Francisco. Don’t remember the vintage, drank it that evening.

Luck of the Irish, as it were. Or at least the Scottish.
